summary: Agent Role Builder role resume_required: Arbitration after 2 consecutive splits: Pushback. `reviewer-codex-r1` returned `reject` with one blocking and two major conceptual groups, and those issues are recoverable specification/governance gaps rather than a non-recoverable validation or execution failure. The draft still departs from approved source authority on arbitration and update/fix lifecycle behavior, contradicts itself on canonical artifact identity, and does not yet define a complete governed artifact surface.
status_reason: Arbitration after 2 consecutive splits: Pushback. `reviewer-codex-r1` returned `reject` with one blocking and two major conceptual groups, and those issues are recoverable specification/governance gaps rather than a non-recoverable validation or execution failure. The draft still departs from approved source authority on arbitration and update/fix lifecycle behavior, contradicts itself on canonical artifact identity, and does not yet define a complete governed artifact surface.

participant_records.verdict: {"verdict":"reject","conceptual_groups":[{"id":"group-1","summary":"Board-governance semantics are invented or overconstrained relative to the request schema and runtime behavior.","severity":"major","findings":[{"id":"f1","description":"The role summary says every governed run requires one live Codex leader, but the request schema allows the leader provider to be `codex`, `claude`, or `gemini`; only reviewer pairing is constrained to Codex+Claude.","source_section":"<role>"},{"id":"f2","description":"The draft says freeze requires every reviewer to approve and that any disagreement keeps the run non-frozen, but the board contract/runtime freezes on no blocking or major issues and treats `conditional` as non-blocking. This changes the actual freeze rule.","source_section":"<guardrails> / <steps>"},{"id":"f3","description":"`changes_required` is referenced as a governing verdict even though the reviewer contract only defines `approved`, `conditional`, and `reject`. That adds semantics not present in the review protocol.","source_section":"<guardrails>"}],"redesign_guidance":"Align all board rules to the actual contract: require Codex+Claude reviewer pairs, do not hardcode a Codex-only leader unless the schema does, and express freeze criteria in materiality terms (`approved` or `conditional` allowed, no blocking/major unresolved items). Remove verdict names that do not exist in the protocol."},{"id":"group-2","summary":"Authority and filesystem boundary are not stated in an enforceable way.","severity":"major","findings":[{"id":"f4","description":"The authority chain presents static docs as operative subordination, but the bootstrap and architecture say the COO controller governs turns and runtime rules come from the memory engine. As written, authority is unclear about what is live governance versus reference evidence.","source_section":"<authority>"},{"id":"f5","description":"`Filesystem writes outside role package directories` is too vague for this workflow. The tool writes both canonical role artifacts and run-scoped artifacts (`drafts/`, `rounds/`, `runtime/`, `result.json`), so the allowed write surface is not actually defined.","source_section":"<authority>"}],"redesign_guidance":"State runtime authority in the same order the project does: COO-governed execution first, memory-engine rules as live governance when applicable, and docs as reference authority. Then define the write boundary precisely: canonical role package directory plus the current governed run directory and its known artifact subpaths."},{"id":"group-3","summary":"Artifact lifecycle is incomplete and pathing is inconsistent with the required outputs.","severity":"major","findings":[{"id":"f6","description":"Canonical artifacts in `<outputs>` are listed as bare filenames, but the required outputs are canonical paths under `tools/agent-role-builder/role/`. The self-check failures correctly show that the authoritative canonical paths are missing from the package definition.","source_section":"<outputs> / Self-check issues"},{"id":"f7","description":"The draft mixes canonical promotion and run-directory emission without clearly separating them by terminal state. Step 5 says decision log and board summary are written always, `<outputs>` says they are canonical on freeze, and `<completion>` refers generically to an `output directory`, leaving lifecycle and location ambiguous.","source_section":"<steps> / <outputs> / <completion>"}],"redesign_guidance":"Use exact canonical paths for frozen artifacts and explicitly distinguish them from run-directory artifacts. Define, by terminal state, which artifacts are always written in the run directory, which are conditional (`pushback`, `resume_required`), and which are promoted to `tools/agent-role-builder/role/` only on freeze."},{"id":"group-4","summary":"Scope text has redundant exclusions that weaken boundary clarity.","severity":"minor","findings":[{"id":"f8","description":"`Application workflow creation` appears twice in the not-in-scope list, once bare and once with clarification. The duplication looks accidental and makes the exclusion less crisp.","source_section":"<scope>"},{"id":"f9","description":"`Application runtime orchestration` is excluded twice with slightly different phrasing, which adds noise rather than a sharper boundary.","source_section":"<scope>"}],"redesign_guidance":"Collapse duplicated exclusions into single lines with the clarifying parenthetical kept once."}],"residual_risks":["The arbitration path is still underdefined at the role level; if kept, it should be tied to the governing review rules instead of free text in Step 4.","Hardcoding the exact tag set in Step 3 may become brittle if the governed tag schema evolves and the role does not name the authority for that schema.","If canonical versus run-artifact locations remain ambiguous, future self-checks and promotions will continue to disagree even when the prose looks complete."],"strengths":["The core mission is clear: create, update, and fix agent role packages through governed multi-LLM review.","The draft correctly treats invented semantics as pushback-worthy and keeps authority expansion out of scope.","The lifecycle shape is close to usable because it already distinguishes canonical, evidence, and internal artifacts at a high level."]}

{"status":"pushback","rationale":"Status is pushback because `reviewer-codex-r0` returned `reject` with three material major conceptual groups, and those issues remain unresolved. `reviewer-claude-r0` was only `conditional` with minor/suggestion feedback, which does not block by itself but also does not override a material reject. The defects are revisable role-definition and governance inconsistencies, not a non-recoverable execution failure, so `blocked` is not warranted.","unresolved":["Board-governance semantics are not source-aligned: the draft hardcodes a Codex-only leader, requires unanimous approval to freeze, and references an unsupported `changes_required` verdict instead of the approved materiality-based freeze model where `approved` or `conditional` can freeze if no blocking/major issues remain.","Authority and write-boundary semantics are not enforceable: operative authority order is unclear, static docs are treated like live superiors, and the writable surface is not precisely defined for both canonical role artifacts and run-scoped artifacts.","Artifact path and lifecycle semantics are inconsistent: `<outputs>` does not declare the required canonical `tools/agent-role-builder/role/...` paths, the self-check already flags those outputs as missing, and Step 5 / `<completion>` do not define one authoritative lifecycle across frozen and non-frozen outcomes."],"improvements_applied":["Step 3 explicitly enumerates the required XML tag set, which tightens the self-check target.","The draft separates outputs into canonical artifacts, evidence artifacts, and internal run artifacts instead of one undifferentiated list.","The workflow already distinguishes leader drafting, self-check, board review, and terminal resolution, with reviewer-pair and provenance guardrails present."],"arbitration_used":false,"arbitration_rationale":null}

{"status":"pushback","rationale":"Pushback. `reviewer-codex-r1` returned `reject` with one blocking and two major conceptual groups, and those issues are recoverable specification/governance gaps rather than a non-recoverable validation or execution failure. The draft still departs from approved source authority on arbitration and update/fix lifecycle behavior, contradicts itself on canonical artifact identity, and does not yet define a complete governed artifact surface.","unresolved":["Align governance semantics to approved sources: arbitration is source-defined as minor-only `frozen_with_conditions` accepted by the invoker, and the draft's update/fix decision-log append / board-summary replace rules are not source-backed against the cited architecture or current implementation.","Make canonical artifact identity internally consistent across markdown, contract, and compliance evidence: Step 2 requires exact canonical promoted paths, but `package_files` still uses basename-only values and the ARB-008 compliance claim therefore overstates correctness.","Complete or narrow the artifact lifecycle surface: the contract currently omits required per-round audit artifacts, failure/bug-report artifacts, and component-governance artifacts such as `rulebook.json` and `review-prompt.json`, despite claiming authoritative governance over board-review execution and audit evidence."],"improvements_applied":["Removed the earlier invented board-contract pieces around Codex-only leadership and unsupported reviewer verdict names; reviewer-pair hard gates and the approved `approved`/`conditional`/`reject` verdict set are now explicit.","Made operative authority and governed write boundaries much clearer by separating reference evidence from live authority and naming the canonical role root plus governed run root/subpaths.","Expanded outputs and completion into a more structured canonical-vs-run-scoped lifecycle model with explicit promoted paths and conditional run artifacts."],"arbitration_used":false,"arbitration_rationale":null}

open_questions: Board-governance semantics are not source-aligned: the draft hardcodes a Codex-only leader, requires unanimous approval to freeze, and references an unsupported `changes_required` verdict instead of the approved materiality-based freeze model where `approved` or `conditional` can freeze if no blocking/major issues remain.

Authority and write-boundary semantics are not enforceable: operative authority order is unclear, static docs are treated like live superiors, and the writable surface is not precisely defined for both canonical role artifacts and run-scoped artifacts.

Artifact path and lifecycle semantics are inconsistent: `<outputs>` does not declare the required canonical `tools/agent-role-builder/role/...` paths, the self-check already flags those outputs as missing, and Step 5 / `<completion>` do not define one authoritative lifecycle across frozen and non-frozen outcomes.

Align governance semantics to approved sources: arbitration is source-defined as minor-only `frozen_with_conditions` accepted by the invoker, and the draft's update/fix decision-log append / board-summary replace rules are not source-backed against the cited architecture or current implementation.

Make canonical artifact identity internally consistent across markdown, contract, and compliance evidence: Step 2 requires exact canonical promoted paths, but `package_files` still uses basename-only values and the ARB-008 compliance claim therefore overstates correctness.

Complete or narrow the artifact lifecycle surface: the contract currently omits required per-round audit artifacts, failure/bug-report artifacts, and component-governance artifacts such as `rulebook.json` and `review-prompt.json`, despite claiming authoritative governance over board-review execution and audit evidence.
